title: Common Phrasal Verbs
description: Master the most essential phrasal verbs to sound natural and understand native English speakers.

items.example: Don't give up — you're almost there!
I've been looking for my keys for half an hour.
I found out the truth by reading her diary.

items.front: run out of (use all / have none left)
items.example: We've run out of coffee — can you pick some up?
Please take off your shoes at the door. The plane took off on time.
I came across this old photo while cleaning the attic.

items.back: aparecer / subir volumen vs rechazar / bajar volumen
items.example: He turned up two hours late. Turn down the music — it's too loud!

items.front: get on with (proceed / have a relationship)
items.example: I get on really well with my colleagues. Just get on with your work!

items.front: bring up (raise a child / mention a topic)
items.example: She was brought up in a small town. Don't bring up the argument at dinner.

items.prompt: She ___ trying after failing the test three times — she kept going.
items.explanation: 'Didn't give up' = didn't stop trying. She kept going despite failures.

items.prompt: We ___ milk — can someone go to the shop?
items.explanation: 'Run out of' = have none left. We've run out of milk.

items.prompt: I ___ an old school friend while shopping yesterday — what a coincidence!
items.explanation: 'Come across' = meet or find unexpectedly. I came across an old friend.

items.prompt: He was ___ by his grandparents after his parents moved abroad.
items.explanation: 'Brought up' = raised (a child). He was brought up by his grandparents.

items.prompt: She was late to the interview because the bus ___ 30 minutes late.
items.explanation: 'Turned up' = arrived. The bus turned up 30 minutes late.

activities.title: Complete with the correct phrasal verb

items.sentence: Don't ___ up — you're nearly at the finish line!
I've been ___ for my passport everywhere. I can't find it.
We ___ out of fuel in the middle of nowhere. It was a nightmare.
I ___ across a beautiful old bookshop while wandering the streets.
How did you ___ out that she was planning a surprise party?
